channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: >tfw fell for the NEET meme how the fuck can you guys endure this? — 39 replies — 156.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>tfw fell for the NEET meme how the fuck can you guys endure this?

Open thread • Replies: 39 • Updated: 2026-04-26 03:45 UTC

OP

>tfw fell for the NEET meme
how the fuck can you guys endure this? it's so boring and depressing, I unironically miss my wagecuck 9-5

Early replies (first 3)

62156768
I would unironically wagecuck still if I was able to take vacation whenever I want. I don't enjoyed scheduling things like that or requiring permission.

62156775
>>62156751
I became a neet because of crippling social anxiety, not out of choice.
I suspect many are the same.

62156779
>>62156751
see you fell for it without having any money, I did that once and then realized that it costs money to do anything other than sit in front of a screen all day every day

Depending on where you live, even going out for a walk will cost you bus fare or gas to get to a decent park. Never felt so trapped, now if I had money to go out and do things like camping or fishing or even just cook nice meals then I would have been a lot happier

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62156868
>>62156751
Steam drugs and the hub you filthy fucking casual tourist. Some of us are actually disabled neets.

Faggit.

62156871
>>62156751

You're boring and don't have any hobbies.

62156873
>>62156751
youre right but wagecucking is worse for me

62156885
>>62156751
>NEET
>free time to develop hobbies/skills
You gotta think about it like money: it doesn't get up and do everything for you. (You) gotta put it to work.
>contract ended = neet until new job
>decided to do investment research
>found dip 8 months later the did 10,000x
It's kind of like virtual prospecting but on that note I've seen someone in metals/commodities generals that does prospecting and has advice in the archives for how to look for signs of gold deposits. Could be worth looking into.
Back to virtual stuff, while I was fortunate due to looking around, if I'd been looking around more often I'd have seen Asteroid Shib sitting at an ideal buy-in price during March. Some say it can run further but I haven't done the research, my point is that you can only find stuff that can greatly multiply the initial amount, if you're looking around in the first place.

62156887
NEETing for a short period of time is no issue. HOWEVER, the job market in burgerland literally doesn't forgive taking ANY job gap whatsoever, most especially in 2026's abysmal job market where every job posted has 500+ applicants each within literal minutes of them being posted.
> but firm handshake bro!
Yeah, and then they'll tell you to apply online and join the pile.

62156893
>>62156871
this

62156899
>>62156816
You're supposed to invest that money you tard

62156903
>>62156751
Shalom, Rabbi

62156904
>>62156751
Go to your local park and play some b-ball with the rest of the salary-mooching section 8 brothas, you fucking cancer

62156905
>>62156794
are you me? that's what I'm doing right now
I had 65K last October
Now down to 35K savings
Applied to 400 jobs
it's gonna turn around any minute now
finance / accounting Excel guru by the way I'll run your entire accounting and finance
MBA no CPA hire me please
>no homo

62156906
>>62156751
You were buckbroken, anon. Elites don't "miss" work kek. Only buckbroken wagebrains do.

62156917
>>62156751
“Let's suppose that you were able every night to dream any dream that you wanted to dream. And that you could, for example, have the power within one night to dream 75 years of time. Or any length of time you wanted to have. And you would, naturally as you began on this adventure of dreams, you would fulfill all your wishes. You would have every kind of pleasure you could conceive. And after several nights of 75 years of total pleasure each, you would say "Well, that was pretty great." But now let's have a surprise. Let's have a dream which isn't under control. Where something is gonna happen to me that I don't know what it's going to be. And you would dig that and come out of that and say "Wow, that was a close shave, wasn't it?" And then you would get more and more adventurous, and you would make further and further out gambles as to what you would dream. And finally, you would dream ... where you are now. You would dream the dream of living the life that you are actually living today.”

62157054
>>62156887
>>62156905
See if ULINE has anything that'll work for you. Most people forget they even exist.

62157069
>>62156906
This.
I copefarm reddit and all the working class love the feeling of being between jobs. They say they feel so free and it is a humiliating handing out resumes and going to interviews.

62157072
>>62156917
Kys Alan watts

62157101
>Everyday I lie awake at night and ask myself..what if this is it? What if I don't make it?

62157117
>>62156838
wish i could study like i were back in college again, i have forgotten everything. once i earned an A in calculus, i was so sharp, and i swore that i would keep it going. that was thirty years ago.

62157126
>>62156779
Who the fuck is too poor to afford a bus what is this larp. If you can't ride a bus how do you have a roof over your head

62157134
>>62156917
>if you eat great food every day, eventually you'll be so bored you'll start eating shit
>if you sit on a comfy chair every day, eventually you'll be so bored you'll start sitting with a knife up your ass
I don't think that's how boredom works

62157204
>>62157117
I hear you. I don’t have the time nor the energy like I did back then; waging just drains a man for a pittance.
>>62156917
I fucked up so many times. It would be nice to wake up and it all fades away.

62157215
>>62157134
lmao!
Thats pretty much what wagies say
I just told some guy
>i am 35 and I never had a job
You know what he said with a sneer on his face?
>thats not the flex you think it is
LOL

62157256
>>62156905
>>62156794
how do you guys feel about AI

62157293
>>62157256
I WISH I HAD HELD ON TO MY MICRON LETF AT 50 BUCKS IN DECEMBER

NOW THAT IT'S 250 BUCKS

62157298
>>62157134
>>62157215
to be fair if you gave me a million bucks/financial security I would 'wagie' by trying to become a corporate finance lawyer, because I would want to have 10 million and 100 million and be a CEO. Because I can't stand being a broke bitch. Give me 70 years so I can become charlie munger or warren buffet. Fuck this poorfag shit.

62157475
>>62157298
None of the two people you mentioned came from rags, bro. Lol.

62157483
>>62157475
Yes and Trump got a million dollar loan

But even if he lost the loan his dad was worth half a billion anyway so he had no real risk. My point is if you give me financial security so that I don't feel like putting 100k into the market could bankrupt me or make me, I would be so much more than I am. But the system actively tries to fuck you in the ass.

Like playing as Wallachia in Victoria 3, you spend the entire game trying to absorb Moldova and liberate yourself and get great powers to help you fuck up Austria to get your wider territory back. By the time you're a great power it's already 1900 and the game is almost over.

62157525
>>62157483
2.5% of Americans have 1m

62157542
>>62157126
Most NEETs live really tight. They act smug about it here but they need to budget everything. I never fully could go NEETing because here and there I always did some tutoring to have some money for shit beyond food. I also missed out all the possible moonshots from 2012 onwards, including triple digit BTC and double digit ETH, single digit AMD, etc. because I never had money. In hindsight I could have skipped a computer upgrade back in 2019 but still it wouldn't made me a millionaire.
Dating also becomes impossible after 27. Girls start asking what you do for a living, and answering "I'm a college student" and "I live with my parents" is no longer good for them.
>>62156871
What hobbies can someone have when living with parents that set the rules in a small house? Painting? Writing? Cycling?

62157561
>>62157542
> and "I live with my parents" is no longer good for them.
If they know you don’t come from a rich family, yeah.

62157574
>>62157542
>Most NEETs live really tight. They act smug about it here but they need to budget everything
Who doesn’t have a budget? Rich and poors have a budget. Hell, it is probably only the rich that even have a budget.

item.title: BITCOIN CRACKED WITH QUANTUM COMPUTER — 21 replies — 84.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
BITCOIN CRACKED WITH QUANTUM COMPUTER

Open thread • Replies: 21 • Updated: 2026-04-26 03:40 UTC

OP

Uh oh STINKY..BITCOIN...

Early replies (first 3)

62156686
>>62156684
UHHHH OHHHHH BAGGIES!!!

62156688
>>62156684
NICE TWITTER AD!

62156690
someone needs to build a segregated internet that indians cant join

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62156698
>>62156690
fuck you bloody

62156703
>>62156698
BENCHOD

62156732
Algorand bros, we are so fucking back!

62156738
>>62156703
basterd!

62156793
>>62156684
if that were true every single website and bank account would have been hacked by now.

62156864
>>62156684
Short it with 10x leverage then.

62156954
>cryptonobler
one of the most heinous slop accounts, shame on you OP.

>search space of 32,767
Nothingburger as expected.
My phone can crack that in two seconds.

62156982
>>62156684
A QUANTUM COMPOOPER JUST FLEW OVER MY HOUSE

62156988
>>62156684
you don't need a quantum computer for this though, arguably wouldn't even be the best option. a single one of the datacenters used for ai training is more than powerful enough to brute force and validate a gazillion private keys to find a wallet with something in it

62156998
>>62156684
Why would you be invested in an unbacked digital token? You really gotta own tangible appreciating assets, the Schwab tribe will hate you, but so be it.

62157303
lmao
Nic is probably sweating cold right now
everyone is making fun of his project 11 scam
lost essentially ALL is rep in this fraud

62157306
>>62156988
>>62156998
these are bot posts, right? you can't legally be this retarded unless you're some bangladeshi 4'7'' guptacel

62157318
I'm all in on margined gold miners tho

62157322
>>62156732
I'm gonna coooom

62157396
wow he even included a screenshot of some packages being installed... seems legit

62157408
>open Ubuntu
>run apt update
>screenshot it
>post it to fucking 4chan of all places and try to claim that a real, successful quantum attack on Bitcoin just happened
How mentally retarded do you need to be to do this? This doesn't even qualify as bait, it's just fucking stupid.

62157522
>ethbox as hostname and user
noticed that efereum retards were trying to do quantum fud on bitcoin. even got some irrelevant google workers to write a "white paper" on the danger of quantum computing (in like 2040).
here i am hoping quantum actually progresses so that all those old satoshi era coins that havent moved at all can start to circulate again.

62157559
>>62156684
Yet bitcoin isn't crashing hmmmm

item.title: >zero investing experience >doesn't even work in finance >worked for SBF >fired from OpenAI for behavior unbecoming >giv… — 60 replies — 83.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
>zero investing experience >doesn't even work in finance >worked for SBF >fired from OpenAI for behavior unbecoming >giv…

Open thread • Replies: 60 • Updated: 2026-04-26 03:07 UTC

OP

>zero investing experience
>doesn't even work in finance
>worked for SBF
>fired from OpenAI for behavior unbecoming
>given billions of dollars to play with just...because

goycattle really have no idea what is going on in the world

Early replies (first 3)

62149920
>>62149919
if you bought NVDA youd be retired. just get better.

62149927
>>62149920
the man in the picture has puts on nvidia

stay confused and goy

62149991
>>62149919
I hate how all rich white dudes have the same long horse face, 6’2ish, lanky, and probably a long penis to boot.

They always look like this guy and I can’t fucking stand it

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62151842
>>62149991
Tall white dudes don't have long dicks, it's usually medium height white dudes. Tall white dudes do have big balls though.

62151844
>>62150489
>Patrilineal name transfer
>Matrilineal ethnicity transfer

62152079
>>62151827
>Aryans
>Avital Balwit
Balwit is eastern euro jewish.

>Aschenbrenner
Coal burner.
It's like pottery.

62152134
>>62149991
He is a homosexual German, though.

62152257
>>62149991
>sees a White person
>seethes and talks about his penis

Serious question: why are brown/black/yellow people like this?

62152274
>>62150535
It's very easy to tell Yiddish names from actual German names if you know German. Jews were not allowed to learn crafts so they never have names like
>Schneider
>Müller
>Jäger
Instead, they usually combined words of nature to create surnames
>Rosenberg (rose mountain)
>Goldfarb (golden color)
>Grünspan (green chip)
The presence of -'stein' is no clear indicator either. Is it the name of an actual place like
>Regenstein
>Wallenstein
>Fürstenstein
? Then it's not a jew. If it's something like
>Goldstein
>Feinstein
it's clearly a jew.

62152340
CCI is what actually measures how main street is doing. How the fuck does trump still have plebe support at this point?

https://www.advisorperspectives.com/dshort/updates/2026/03/31/two-measures-of-consumer-attitudes-march-2026

62152354
you should be glad he's an actual german and not a kike.
>zero investing experience
see below
>doesn't even work in finance
see below again
>worked for SBF
here's the finance experience you claim doesn't exist. retard. he was smart enough to see the writing on the walls and got out while he still could
>fired from OpenAI for behavior unbecoming
so he pissed off a top kike? whats the problem here?
>given billions of dollars to play with just...because
so he pissed off a top kike? and went off and did his own thing with apparently great success? whats the problem here?

62152374
>>62152354
>What's the problem here
The problem is I want a billion dollars and do not want other people to have a billion dollars. This kid can get fucked, I should be getting prime teen pussy not this fucking zoomer faggot

62152385
>>62152257
My exes friend had a bf like this. He had the long horse face sunken eyes and a 12 inch penis. I had to dump my ex because she was constantly trying to pursue him. It got to the point where our mutual friend was asking me to tell her to stop hitting on him.

It was unreal man. This guy mogged me to death.

62152492
>>62149919
I wonder if he used AI to influence his decisions... The answer is yes, he did.

62152567
>>62150485
How can you be a virgin at 6’.5’’, even as a lanklet you should still easily get dates as being tall makes up for ugliness, also you got big dick so don’t be insecure about it. Girth matters most and you got girth

62152773
>>62152385
Grim

62152801
>>62152274
This. Anons need to learn the difference between jews and Germans fr fr no cap. It's not even hard once you learn the patterns. Also everyone ITT thinking he looks jewish is also retarded. Read some legit nationalsocialist era race science before you pass judgment. This snownigger has a German name and looks white, just not with the best physiognomy (he is a rich libshit who cavorts with jews after all). I had a friend growing up who looked very similar, also a nerdy libtard type who was 0% kike, just anglo.

62153742
>>62151827
So his fiancee fired him? Lol

62153750
>>62151842
not true i am 6ft but my weiner is like 7 and 5/8 inch on a good day so not that long

62154536
>>62152274
>they never have names like
>>Schneider

62154606
>>62152567
I never tried; went to a college that was 99% male; immediately got a job working 50 hours a week right out of college; no opportunities to meet people/women.
Thanks, bro.
>>62153750
You just confirmed what he was saying.

62154812
>>62152274
>Jews were not allowed to learn crafts
>Schneider is totally ethnic german

62154876
>>62150463
Please get to sleep Sam

62154883
>>62149927
>the man in the picture has puts on nvidia
source?
>>62150001
it could've been fine/interesting until this >>62149991
>>62149919
what the fu ck

62154884
>>62150489
>>>62150009

>Leopold Aschenbrennen
Literally means
>Leopold Ash-burner

Is this like when you call a jew an ovendodger, but for nazis?

62154888
>>62154884
oh were pretending that happened now?

62154889
>>62149991
i look like that and yes our penises are large.

62154891
>>62149991
Reptilian features.

62154894
>>62154812
Find me a JEEEW named that.
You can't, Herr Hitlers ghost is making sure of that!

62154895
>>62149991
This but unironically, the horse face is ugly and too many anons are coping in the replies.
t. white with a good human face

62156876
>>62149920
>doing a free endorsement
Baggies

62157227
>>62152354
>here's the finance experience you claim doesn't exist

He only worked on the philanthropy side, picking causes to give to. Keep defending grifters, clown.

62157496
>>62154884
applause for being only person in thread to type out his long name

item.title: Putting over 6k into Bill Ackmans IPO fund PSUS. — 6 replies — 24.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Putting over 6k into Bill Ackmans IPO fund PSUS.

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-04-26 03:41 UTC

OP

Putting over 6k into Bill Ackmans IPO fund PSUS. Am I going to make it?

Early replies (first 3)

62157100
>>62157099
IPO is when the VC funds cash out

62157110
>>62157100
He has already secured 2.8 billion and institutions will own it. Surely that means it will hit 1000 a share some day like Berkshire

62157430
>>62157099
No

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62157476
>>62157430
Why not

62157502
oh look, this ad again

62157562
>>62157502
It's not an ad faggot. You think I'm bill ackman or something?

item.title: Proof of Work BTFO: Litecoin Bug Allowed Double Spend and Had A Chain Reorg — 12 replies — 18.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Proof of Work BTFO: Litecoin Bug Allowed Double Spend and Had A Chain Reorg

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-04-26 03:11 UTC

OP

A Litecoin bug found today allowed double spend and led to miners having to reorg multiple blocks. PoW is a failed consensus model. Your entire security depends on an assumption that others won't spend more money on attacking the network than miners will defending it, and that those mining will not continue to sell the network out, with Epstein and co. having already permanently neutered it as spendable medium-of-exchange money to transform it into the random evolution of "store of value," controlling the majority of the core devs across a majority of code build and corporatists still controlling a majority of mining. Bitcoin has had a full chain reorg and Nakamoto probabilistic fork choice algo does not give full settlement for this reason. The best incentive is no incentive on networks which are as secure at 1 dollar as they are at 1000 and this is the only model institutions will meaningfully build actual RWA etc. on.
https://www.theblock.co/post/398892/litecoin-rewrites-three-hours-of-history-to-undo-its-first-major-privacy-layer-exploit

Early replies (first 3)

62156962
>>62156949
I am holding eth too.

62156967
>>62156949

62156991
>>62156967
>.gif
dub- dub- dub-
hi! my name is
>what?
hi! my name is
>who?
chic-chicka-slim shady

hi! my name is
>ha?
hi! my name is
>who?
chic-chicka-slim shady

hi! my name is
>what?
hi! my name is
>who?
chic-chicka-slim shady

excuse me!
can I have attention of the class
for one second

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62157048
>>62156991
we're gonna have a problem here

62157083
>>62156949
>Your entire security depends on an assumption that others won't spend more money on attacking the network than miners will defending it, and that those mining will not continue to sell the network out
in this case it was an exploit of the node software itself

62157089
>problem solved before anybody even heard about it
>price unaffected
What is this bullshit? Where is the flash dump?

62157092
>>62157083
>in this case it was an exploit of the node software itself
Which explains why link will never be anything special.

62157234
>>62156949
Soooo….buy bitbean?

62157252
>>62156949
>Your entire security depends on an assumption that others won't spend more money on attacking the network than miners will defending it
I don't own any crypto, but this describes a lot of very important things. Like a physical vault is like that. If someone spends more money than what's used to build/guard the vault, they'll get into the vault.

62157402
>>62157252
Definitely, but this adds a MUCH easier anonymity and custodians are counterparties working on reputation. If JP Morgan vaults get sold out and are uninsured they lose a business worth WAY more than their physical holdings and are personally and institutionally exposed. When proof of work networks are exploited or coopted, there is just a layer of abstraction or investment losers unhappy when the network majority votes to evolve in a way that SCREWS retail and sells out defi values (as has already happened, requiring centralization into super shitty, TURBO-centralized L2s 5o be remotely viable while still being 100x more expensive to use than other networks which scale at L1 with no concentrated risk faster). Bank heists are possible but harder and insured against and much more difficult. Blockchain tech is for sure the future, but PoW is it's proto, beta test origination.

62157467
Litecoin was a coin really loved by 4channellers. Did all the litecoin enjoyers leave?

62157499
>>62156949
>PoW is a failed consensus model
>Bitcoin has had a full chain reorg
>Nakamoto probabilistic fork choice algo does not give full settlement
All of this is actual made up fake bullshit, you are literally saying nothing at all. What happened today with Litecoin has nothing to do with its consensus algorithm. You are a giant piece of shit and I sincerely hope you die soon. My schizo theory is that this is somehow related to Calude's Mythos AI because this shit is really good at finding zero day exploits and from what I can gather this was one.

item.title: Now that a CS degree is worth less than an art degree, what's the new "learn to code"? — 11 replies — 9.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Now that a CS degree is worth less than an art degree, what's the new "learn to code"?

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-04-26 02:41 UTC

OP

Now that a CS degree is worth less than an art degree, what's the new "learn to code"? I've seen people suggest the trades but they pay like shit and have you doing back-breaking work for like $25/hr

Early replies (first 3)

62157292
>>62157269
learn to suck dick.

62157310
>>62157269
Learn to be creative, 100 trillion dollar entertainment industry when the UBI kicks in and everything is vibe coded/AI assisted.

62157319
>>62157310
anything that relies on creativity is more luck-based than anything, you're missing what made coding so great. All you had to do was go to college for 4 years and you'd basically be guaranteed a comfy six figure job. Trying to start an ai business is not the same thing at all

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62157332
>>62157269
"Something something ai"

62157335
>>62157319
I think creativity is something AI doesn't excel at is what I'm saying. The grunt work of doing(in video games for example) coding and modeling 3d shit will be done by AI, but the actual creative part isn't easy for AI. Just my opinion on a scenario that may or may not come to pass. I just think if logically a lot of people get unemployed they will look for escapism. The creative/entertainment space might get much bigger.

62157342
>>62157269
>learn the trades
https://x.com/pnickdurham/status/2047069554917154837

Top professions for 2027:

- influencer
- streamer/gamer
- private butler, maid, groundskeeper
- private security
- gambler
- onlyfans
- street prostitute
- drug dealer
- scam artist

62157347
>>62157269
Learn to plunge, get good unclogging shitters

62157382
>>62157269
> what's the new "learn to code"?
Unironically it’s learn to code

62157406
>>62157269
Learn to wipe old ass
Like this anon said, but one step ahead >>62157347

62157424
learn to cope

62157440
Learn to code, but use those skills to create your own business. There are plenty of bullshit nothing internet business you can automate at least 95% of if you know how to code, they won't make you a millionaire but get a few running and you're looking at decent money.

item.title: help — 16 replies — 9.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
help

Open thread • Replies: 16 • Updated: 2026-04-26 02:09 UTC

OP

I lost 20k out my initial capital of 75k. since 6 weeks shorting BTC.closed half still holding. My only hope trump start bomb iran again. Any secret gems that i can get at least 10x in a week? or anything on polymarket?

Early replies (first 3)

62151162
Why would you short BTC? you understand that Iran takes tolls from ships in BTC or Yuan so this literally proves the utility of bitcoin. Haters will deny this truth, but Bitcoin is an eagerly accepted alternate payment method. Also Saylor has nearly perfected the perpetual motion volatility engine

62151171
>>62151162
>Why would you short BTC?
i trust Bitcoin cycle repeat and global instability

62151185
>>62151148
it crashed at an extreme pace over the past few months, did you really expect another leg down?

And regarding global events, always bet on nothing ever happens, faggot

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62151202
>>62151148
>My only hope trump start bomb iran again
I know making money is a zero sum game and war is maximum profitable but damn it still gets to me that a whole bunch of traders around the world are wishing death upon another nation so their numbers go up.

62151203
>>62151148
>I lost 20k out my initial capital of 75k
>shorting BTC

I love that for you.

62151255
>>62151148
So you wanna lose all of your 75k?

My onle advice is that you market buy LINK and hold

62151340
>>62151148
How the hell happened? I must admit I was also shorting, but it's been such a nice pump I've been able to break even and not lose anything. We're not having any violent candles and we usually dump during the weekend.

Try not to go too crazy with the leverage.

62151344
>>62151148
>help i gambled and lost some money, please tell me which table at the casino to go to next

62151778
>>62151148
>he redeemed
Do not redeem the card sar!

62154519
>>62151148
Lose everything and start a new

62154644
>>62151148
Just buy spot Ether and useful Ethereum tokens. I know you want to copy the genius traders grunting, "Line look like 4 year ago line," but the market is steadily going up.

62154654
>>62151148
I voted for this

62156393
>>62151148
Polymarket won't help you with such high amount of money. I know how to basically guarantee a long term profit on polymarket, but it only works in low liquidity markets.

62156402
I don't know your feel, the most I've ever lost was panic selling bottoms and that was for only 5k in losses back in 2022.

You should consider not shorting after the market has already giga dumped. That would be a smart thing to do.
>secret gems I can 10x in a week
This is how you lose the other 55k.

62157301
>>62151255
LINK will make him lose everything, get into BTC alone that's what you need. I just started staking BTC, looks like native BTCFI is the next trend.

62157346
>>62151148
We can't all make it. Now shut up already, greater fool.

item.title: Hahahahahahaha — 9 replies — 4.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Hahahahahahaha

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-04-26 01:53 UTC

OP

EAT FUCKING SHIT WEATHERXM
Polymarket is signing a deal with nubila due to the Paris blow dryer event (just confirmed in discord)

We won the fucking weather depin war Marco bros
EAT FUCKING SHIT WEATHERXM YOU OVERPRICED PIECE OF SHIT

Early replies (first 3)

62156455
>>62156453
Hahahahahahaha

62156456
>>62156453
I just spent 2 weeks collecting data and training a model on Chongqing weather station for nothing ...

62156462
>>62156456
Hahaha hahaha
Join us Marco bro
You can buy them at home depot
They have a contract with home depot now

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62156469
>>62156462
Feels SO FUCKING GOOD

62156545
>>62156453
Source? what discord?

62156666
>>62156453
What the fuck does some shitcoin have to do with weather? I read a bit about it and it seems like a halfbaked scheme. Reminds me of something else...

62156683
>>62156666
Nubila is partnering with amazon aws for project prometheus.

Guess whose also partnered with Amazon now stinky linky

62157249
it leveled out at ATH
in 2 weeks you weenies are going to be whining no one warned you

62157314
>>62156545
The official nubila discord

item.title: Things that have outperformed BTC since 2021: >The S&P 500 >The Nasdaq >Pokemon cards >Old video games >Vinyl records >O… — 2 replies — 1.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Things that have outperformed BTC since 2021: >The S&P 500 >The Nasdaq >Pokemon cards >Old video games >Vinyl records >O…

Open thread • Replies: 2 • Updated: 2026-04-26 02:39 UTC

OP

Things that have outperformed BTC since 2021:

>The S&P 500
>The Nasdaq
>Pokemon cards
>Old video games
>Vinyl records
>Obscure DVDs and blu rays
>Sealed VHS tapes
>Gold

If you didn't sell above 100k you're retarded, I'm sorry

Early replies (first 3)

62156516
>>62156494
I don't care. Can't buy acid online with "obscure dvds and blu rays".

62157431
>>62156516
Bitcoin can be used to buy drugs whether it's $1 or $1 million

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: Polkadot - Mumbai Warrior Edition — 87 replies — 15.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Polkadot - Mumbai Warrior Edition

Open thread • Replies: 87 • Updated: 2026-04-25 22:15 UTC

OP

Hello my fellow PolkaCurry brothers. We are again here, for another thread where we discuss everything related to DOT, Mumbai (the city of DOT), curry and more. The last thread was a resounding success, we managed to keep it alive for literally 41 days. We got paid 11500 + 5*150 = 12250 DOT for the thread and are now living in a period of enlightenment while eating with METAL spoons. Brothers, we have truly reached enlightenment. WE, the Mumbai Warriors will strike yet again and collect generations worth of money !

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

Early replies (first 3)

62115437
We are eating with our METAL spoons in Mumbai. No more hands my brethren. We eat with out spoons, like proud warriors.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62115624
>>62115424
>>62115437

Still gonna larp as an Indian?

62115793
>>62115437

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62126538
>>62124594
>>62125019
>>62125037
>>62125463
Good job my fellow warriors of Mumbai. Thank you for keeping the thread alive with your comments.

>this is the most demoralizing dot thread
How so my Mumbai brother?

>Way to promote a project then rug people
Yes Ganesha. We play the peoples hearts and then we stab them in their hearts.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62126677
Sol 8>297 Is 37X
Dot 1,15 x37= 42

62126931
>>62126538
Holy shit. Are you actually an autistic retard?

62126975
>>62126931
Kamal, are you getting upset again because your mother took away your metal spoon and ate with it? There is plenty of metal spoons for everyone my young warrior of Mumbai.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62127244
>>62126677
Vrishank, that is really good mathematics. Education is really paying off I see. Very good. You might even become a doctor or a lawyer. Yes, you shall reach to the heavens of nirvana one day my young Vrishank. I, Vishnu Kumar, believe in you and so should you my young Vishnu warrior of the mind chain, my young Mumbai warrior, my young metal spoon owner. Soon, we will have silver spoons for everyone, then gold spoon and after that we reach the nirvana of diamond spoons. The whole of Mumbai will eat.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62127578
>>62126975
Yep. You are officially a retard.

62127673
>>62127578
Vrishank, did you just really say that with the intent you did? Is there anything wrong? Are you not feeling ok?

62128205
>>62126931
>>62127578
you are talking to a bot (and so am I)

62128210
>>62128205
BIP BOP BIP BOP


Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62129040
>>62128205
No, its actual autistic retard

62129456
>>62129040
Impossible, look at the time stamps of the posts

62129518
>>62129040
>>62129456
No, it's actually Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62131415
100$ end of bull run


Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62131658
It is still cheap right now. Don't buy my bags at 25$. You can buy them now at 1.28$. Don't FOMO in at 50$ guys.

62131731
>>62116681
>What exactly does it have that sets it apart from Solona and ETH?
Solana has a success rate associated with it's transactions (see https://solscan.io/analytics). That's kind of fucked up that there's a 10 to 20% chance your tx might not get processed, right? I've never seen a DOT tx fail. DOT doesn't even measure failed tx https://data.parity.io/parachain-stats?selection-mode=include&frequency=daily&relay-chain=polkadot&chains= because there are none. If you submit a tx it'll complete within like 6s to 30s. The longest a tx takes is when you transfer from one corechain to another. So when you bridge (use a HRMP channel) between corechains. And even that never took longer than like 40s from my personal experience. It's bizarre to me that literally up to 20% of the tx simply fail.
Another thing is that SOL literally has more "vote" transactions than anything else; https://solscan.io/analytics#txs . And vote transactions are LITERALLY validator messages to keep the network running. DOT doesn't count that as a tx at all. 60 to 80% of the tx on SOL are from validators to keep the network running. That's insane to think about that you need more tx to run the network than the users use to execute stuff. People are stupid for thinking that SOL processes 3k TPS. More than half of that is just to keep the network running. It's a literal scam number to be honest. It's just to make the normies believe that the network isn't a scam. The number of transactions is completely fake in a sense.
And again, the funny thing is that 1 core of DOT will be able to handle more TPS than the whole of SOL once either JAM or NOMT hit. Maybe even before that once the 500ms Basti blocks hit. If you combine 500ms blocks + JAM + NOMT one core of DOT will be able to probably do anywhere between 2 to 5x more than SOL as a whole and DOT has LITERALLY 100 cores and this will also probably be scaled to more sooner or later. It'll sooner or later scale to about 300 to 500 cores.

62131777
>>62116681
>>62131731
The 300-500 cores are after the network upgrades to 1000 validators from 600 and after a few more future optimizations. But DOT can then scale further by adding more relay chains. So, currently there is 1 relay chain that connects all the corechains (while they also have direct connections through the HRMP channels) and this relay chain has 600 active validators at any given time. At some point DOT could scale further by having multiple relay chains and separate validators on each relay chain and then having a "relay chain" that connects the relay chains. Sure, this would fragment liquidity in a sense but you could still transfer assets between the relay chains in probably like 30s or so... So in a sense the fragmented liquidity would be a non-problem. DOT is truly scalable while other chains simply are not. DOT covered all the problems because Gavin used to develop ETH and noticed "OMG, this won't work out" and then he left ETH in order to develop DOT. DOT is ETH done right in a sense. ETH is just BTC with smart contracts and DOT is an actual solution to all the problems that plague ETH and all the other smart contract platforms.

62135297
100$ end of bull run

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62137662
Soon we shall reach the nirvana of 75$ my Mumbai brothers. Then we shall eat with silver spoons.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62139771
You are not the body, you are not the mind, you are not your emotions. You are the silent DOT in the infinity of consciousness. You are just a wave in the ocean of consciousness that forgot it is part of the ocean. For when rememberance dawns, the mystery is not solved. It is lived.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62141372
My brethren, the time is nigh. We're break out. The wave of consciousness is breaking free from the ocean floor.

62143550
Any second now BTC will erupt into a storm that has never been seen before. DOT will soon start it's ascension to the heavens for 75$+. We, the Mumbai warriors, shall eat with silver spoons very soon. The time is coming my warriors. You still have to to buy a big fat bag of DOT before you get priced out.

62143825
>>62129518
>>62131415
>>62131658
>>62131731
>>62131777
>>62135297
>>62139771
>>62143550

This is sad. Also stop larping as an Indian and perhaps you'll get engagement

62144010
>>62143825
>This is sad.
My brother, the sadness is in your own heart. Not mine. You are destroying yourself.

>Also stop larping as an Indian and perhaps you'll get engagement
My brother, I am the one and only Vishnu Kumar. Born and raised in India. I am brown as the mud itself. There is no denying it. I am a true Mumbai warrior. I am a proud Indian.
Also Panjali, didn't people teach you that questions are fundamental for a healthy conversation. My young Panjali, I am not here for engagement. If you asked I would've told you so. Oh young Panjali. You still have a long way ahead of you. If accept, I let go, I move on and I forgive you young Panjali.

Thank you for you kind bump of the thread Panjali. I shall later come to your house and give you your share of the profits.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62146419
My fellow Mumbai warriors. We are all already enlightened, we just need to uncover it. DOT is already the best chain, people just need to realize it. They're still looking at the shadow of their light and misidentifying it for themselves. Soon they shall become aware of it and pivot towards the light.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62148896
100$ end of bull run. Remember my Mumbai warriors. It is still cheap as dirt itself. You want to buy now, before you the GIGA ULTRA MEGA pump happens and you get priced out.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62149307
>>62148896
And then have this larping indian short you

62151325
>>62149307
Pssssstttttt Kamal. Do you want to eat with your hands again or do you want the Mumbai warriors to eat like true kings? With spoons? Young warrior Kamal, you have still much to learn.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62153932
100$ end of bull run

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62155237
My brothers... nirvana is near, it's just around the corner. Stack up on DOT right now or get priced out.

62156822
My Mumbai brethren. I've been informed that the Palk Strait will be closed by the Mumbai warriors. We will now toll all ships and they'll have to pay the toll in DOT.

item.title: DO YOU KNOW ANYONE WHO LOST MONEY ON MEMECOINS — 26 replies — 11.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
DO YOU KNOW ANYONE WHO LOST MONEY ON MEMECOINS

Open thread • Replies: 26 • Updated: 2026-04-26 01:33 UTC

OP

DO YOU KNOW ANYONE WHO LOST MONEY ON MEMECOINS

Early replies (first 3)

62154328
Yeah

62154329
>>62154323
Almost everyone lost money on memecoins just like how she lost all her gains

62154342
>>62154323
I want her to squeeze my head like a pumpkin in between her thighs, if you catch my meaning.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62154347
>>62154323
I know a guy. I also knew a guy who lost his money on futures. Same story for both. Decided they'd rather day trade from a bar than get a real job. Wounds up broke, alone, and alcoholics.

62154361
>>62154323
memecoins are made for losing money. you have to balance out your capital gains somehow

62154373
>>62154323
Me. Is this a trick question? This is /biz/, most people here lost money on memecoins

62154377
>>62154361
This. You need to get some losses out of your system. Need to get a cheap thrill once in a while with some retarded small bets so that you stay rational with your core folio

62154462
>>62154347
>>62154329
>>62154328
>>62154373
are you guys being serious or are you trolling?

Almost everyone on x that I see is still hyped on memecoins
Can you share more stories? I'm not part of gen z and don't really know what it's like on the ground for you guys

62154473
>>62154323
>yeah lost money last few days
>still losing
>she's cute ngl
>arms are a bit much not my thing

62154481
I know him, and he is me.

62154731
>>62154323
My fren boat trump coin at quite high 2 digit price, i told him to sell the first dead cat but he didnt....

62154753
>>62154462
Yeah

62154757
>>62154323
No I don't ever lose money

62154833
>>62154323
if i encounter anyone who speaks of memecoins i un know them

62154933
>>62154323

62155966
>>62154323
NW needed to date her?

62156010
>>62154323
>>62154342
>>62154473
least attractive sex wrestler. here is a real woman worth rasslin with.

62156018
>>62154323
No, but everyone I know lost money on coins with utility and fundamentals.

62156163
>>62154323
Yes. I have a friend on Facebook who ate shit when bitcoin crashed.

62156176
>>62156010

62156269
>>62156163
Remember that one dude who bet like 6 BTC on Trump winning the election? I thought he was a genius because it seemed like a sure bet, free money! He never did post a follow up...

62156760
>>62156176
ywnwat (you will never wrestle a tomboy)

62157005
>>62154323
I'm down 20k on memes.

62157144
>>62154462
This is a crypto board, why are you even here if you're not trading crypto. There's nothing to tell, I trade crypto and once in a while I flip a shitcoin for fun and generally lose money. There's your story
>>62156018
This too, and this one actually hurts. Kleros would've been so much more expensive if it didn't actually exist and function

62157186
I made a lot of money with memecoins

62157264
no, i never bought memecoins. I only invest in stocks. I did buy a small amount of bitcoin once that i exchanged for monero to buy acid.

item.title: Why the FUCK is life so fucking difficult (particularly the financial aspect of it). — 52 replies — 10.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why the FUCK is life so fucking difficult (particularly the financial aspect of it).

Open thread • Replies: 52 • Updated: 2026-04-25 22:46 UTC

OP

Why the FUCK is life so fucking difficult (particularly the financial aspect of it).

Early replies (first 3)

62155115
>>62155108
it's the way capitalism works, it's an annihilationist force that is counter to life. it will continue until we're all dead and earth has been completely liquidated. I dont' know why we do this to ourselves

>uncleted.jpg

62155117
LUCK is the biggest factor in life.

62155122
>>62155115
Just like cancer

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62155543
>>62155163
this, capitalism is an egregore. its a force that exists as something greater than the sum of its parts (human brains exchanging labor value). it has grown beyond us and will one day devour us

62155588
>>62155530
Stiffle competition.

62155599
The ages don't line up with what you biologically want to do
>As a kid / teenager
This is where you have the most TVM to benefit you, but you can't have a job and if you got one you're just buying a gaming computer and working part-time
>20's
This is when you have good TVM to benefit from but you're just starting in whatever field, or possibly still in school. A natural human doesn't feel like planning for 40 years in the future here, he just wants to make babies and do cool things.
>Late 20's approaching 30's
This is where you feel like you have to start a business or build something or get married - this happens universally to everyone who still has their natural human instincts. At some point you just see what you needed to do in the past to be successful today, and now it's up to yourself from the past to have the capital to make whatever you want to do now work, but they don't have the same motives as you.
>>62155173
Early 20's, not going all the way to 30's with this.
If you spent like 5 years moneymaxxing and just investing as much as possible that would pretty much set you up for life even in a relatively low paying job, even with a mcjob if you just live with your parents and buy rental properties.
>>62155198
>you could literally live with your parents, collect welfare and then go all in on stocks while eating chicken tendies. how is it difficult? you're lucky you weren't born during the middle ages
Yup, more kids need to be told this. Live with your parents, work 5 years investing everything, you've just saved 20 or 30 years of work later.
It's really simple but instead we have these people told they need to go 100,000 in debt and blow 4-6 years of their life studying, when instead they could make 100,000 in that time frame that's all invested.

62155603
>>62155108
I made 7 million dollars slinging shitcoins and I have a tested IQ of 104. Might be a skill issue.

62155607
>>62155603
You can't even retire on 7 million these days. That's at best 5 years of retirement with today's inflation.

62155612
You feel difficult brcauze you don’t have. Little sister like me who can let me breeding press her everyday when my bag is full.

62155614
>>62155607
You can pseudo-retire on 100k if you live with parents and take up a couple hundred in odd jobs per month. Not a true retirement, more like a "work a day a week" thing.

62155675
>>62155108
in the past (pre 2020) it used to be cause people didnt know how to invest and bought shit they didnt need. now its wealth inequality, they're literally siphoning all producitivity gains

62155705
I find it easy to save and invest, but it’s extraordinarily difficult to find an opportunity, go all-in with size, and do a 10x. In this life, you either accept wagecuckery until 62-65, you hypergamble in the market, or you become an entrepreneur.

62155765
>>62155705
Yeah, 10x's are hard but 2x's just happen consistently, so it's more about doing it when you're younger.
A little bit of wagecuckery chasing 2x's goes a long way when you're 20 and those will double like 4 times before retirement.

62155773
>>62155765
The issue is you see an asset that is going parabolic, you say “yeah, this is it” and then it does 2x, 4x, etc. but the very fucking moment you hop in, you’re down 30 percent. Right now, everyone is getting rich on the AI bottleneck trade. If I wasn’t holding bitcoin, I’d just say fuck it and hop in.

62155777
>>62155198
>you could literally live with your parents, collect welfare and then go all in on stocks while eating chicken tendies. how is it difficult? you're lucky you weren't born during the middle ages
lmao literally me except i dont eat tendies
i am currently investing 100% in ftse all world and while it grows well idk if i can escape the neet life with it. also i can also only save up to 10k before the state cuts my neet money
dont even know what i would do with the money if i got lucky

62155782
>>62155773
I've been holding more cash specifically because of the AI stuff, I still invest but I think some kind of downturn is coming and I want to be able to jump at opportunities.
I really would never go all-in on some kind of "ai bottleneck", it's just gambling. Sure, gamble with 25% if you really want to.

Recently houses have been going for 100k in my area (lockport new york because people will say this is fake) and I'm waiting for an even further dip as boomers die off, which has actually been happening because all land owners in my area are 75.

62155861
>>62155782
The outsized returns come with the gambling. Don’t get me wrong, all of my money is in VOO and bitcoin. But it causes deep distress to see people printing off of leveraged sector ETFs. Real estate is sometimes the move. Homes that were $100k in 2002 are now sometimes $1.3m. But I’ve also seen other areas that were the next big thing, and they’ve crabbed for decades.

My take? New York has had net population losses for a while. Buffalo is doing better in recent years, but will it extend to Lockport? Unsure.

62155878
>>62155115
>>62155122
>>62155117
Can't believe you retards haven't figured out the real answer. I'll give you a hint, it's jews.

62155879
>>62155861
>But will it extend to Lockport?
It already has, but in rural areas. My city is in a decline in population and nobody's making any money here and never will, which is where my edge comes in.
Since I'm not 75 and not literally dying to sell off my shit to pay for a new truck I can get along maintaining and renting houses, and it's no big loss to have one I have to sell because I can do basically all cosmetic work myself.

But people are desperate to escape city shitholes so small suburb areas and even lockport have seen buffalo growth.

62155881
>>62155108
Because of genetic determinism and how the modern world works. We have abundance thanks to modern tech and slave labor from third world countries but everyone wants to have access to the wealth. Meanwhile the state simply prints more money making your money worth less. The only way to counter this is by having assets that are not bound to this or scale higher then inflation. We still have law of nature, the fittest aka the most adaptable wins and will be on top, just that most people who would die normally are being dragged on for some reason.

62155923
>>62155108
just stop caring
i havent done laundry in over a year. my apartment is a disaster and i eat like once every 3-4 days out of pure lazyness.
i have no friends never go out other than work.
i take several stimulants to keep my brain on autopilot at work so im not really "there"
i recently lost the will to commute so i just uber it both ways
when i get home i just rot in bed, look at the numbers move and watch oneyplays until i pass out.
sometimes ill play something on my 'eck if im feeling especially motivated.
i just dont care anymore.

62155978
>>62155108
too many people

62156145
>>62155259
>you are framed and jailed for sexual assault,
kek, definitely a thing that happens to normal, well-adjusted people

62156342
>>62155878
jews are huma....oh wait!

62156367
>>62155198
What to invest in tho?
Nothing gives more than 5% annual returns these days unless you do degenerate options gambling

62156382
>>62155130
>where it was financially optimal to take care of your cows and pigs so that they lived happy lives
This is impossible regardless of how well you treat your livestock. Beef cattle are typically slaughtered when they're somewhere between 1-2 years old. Dairy cattle are slaughtered once their milk production starts to decline, around 5 years old.

The natural lifespan of a cow is 20-30 years. You could treat your cattle incredibly well but you're still killing them when they're basically children. Killing a 1-2 year old cow is equivalent to killing a 6-8 year old human. If you were treated very well for the first 8 years of your life before being slaughtered so some alien species could eat you, would you consider that you lived a happy life?

62156385
>>62155108
in order for the few percentage of rich population to enjoy the leisure time, you need a society of people jsut barely getting by so they need to be at work as much as possible making either you or your rich buddies money. all the while keeping you out of their way for a majority of the week.

62156388
>>62156382
I'd have been eaten in 2000, being a wagecuck in 2026.

62156412
>>62155159
seriously though I have this, idk, risk aversion disability where even if something is 99% likely to make big gains if I just hold for a year, I will constantly panic sell and then cry afterwards. I don't know what the fuck is wrong with me. It's happened like 5 times now.

62156445
>>62155923
>oneyplays

dude go get some help tf

62156449
>>62156367
you have to gamble if you want to win. There are too many people on earth for you to make it without taking on risk.

62156515
>>62156476
pretty much nailed it

62156850
>>62155878
Stop noticing

item.title: >nofap >religion >stock tips >meditation >therapy >healing crystals >self-help books >pomodoro >PDFs >affirmations >posi… — 55 replies — 8.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
>nofap >religion >stock tips >meditation >therapy >healing crystals >self-help books >pomodoro >PDFs >affirmations >posi…

Open thread • Replies: 55 • Updated: 2026-04-25 21:21 UTC

OP

>nofap
>religion
>stock tips
>meditation
>therapy
>healing crystals
>self-help books
>pomodoro
>PDFs
>affirmations
>positive thinking
>DCA
>crypto
>"self improvement"

Early replies (first 3)

62143060
>/biz/: Business & Finance

62143073
Different ways of coping. No boomber ever had to read the meditations or go to the gym

62143145
>>62143073
the boomers who did DCA have generational wealth now

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62145005
I've read
>how to win friends and influence people
>art of war
>12 rules by peterson
>48 laws of power
>atomic habits
>the art of not giving a fuck
>the art of the deal
>meditations of auruelius(stoic)
>4 hour work week
>the secret
>rich dad poor dad and the sequel

And yet my life still sucks. And before you say "but anon your'e supposed to do the things the books say to do not just read them!" well I have. For example richdad poordad says to borrow money from your friends and buy a house, flip it and then give them back their money-the exact amount they let you borrow. Well I asked all my friends for money and NONE of them gave me any. Only one even asked what it was for and when I told him he laughed in my face and said "You have to know how to flip a house to flip a house."

What the fuck

62145007
>>62145001
>>62144995
>generally pretty quick to reply
The irony. Also you have to be 18+ to post here

62145009
>>62144501
>the meaning to this life is uhhhh if you're a good boy and follow this book, you'll have an infinitely long life after this one, 100% guaranteed, and in that life everything will be great, okay?

62145415
>>62143054
>>nofap
Difficult to do, confers immediate health and psychological benefits, and every normie has a bizarre level hatred for it.

That should tell you something.

62145428
>>62144238
Of course stoicism is a cuck mentality. It's a philosophy of decadence. No Greek during the peak of their civilization would have created something like stoicism the same way no boomber would have been interested in the meditations by marcus aurelius.

When the real world becomes too painful, many people retire and create ways of coping.

62145464
>>62145428
Real stoicism is a an "emptying"/devotion of yourself to higher Being. This is a living, communicative path that taps you into energetic circuits and correspondence. Stoicism as most people know it isn't transformative in any higher sense.

62145495
>>62145464
Well, I meant decadence in the societal sense. Individuals live in a society. Stoicism was born exactly when the greek dream ended and that's why it became so popular. Same way as Epicureism.

And that's why it is so goddamn popular now and you have depressed 21 yo kids talking about stoicism. It has become a meme. You're depressed? Read The Meditations and go to the gym, bro. The real world has become painful and people have to find ways to deal with the real world.

ps. By the way, Seneca moral writings are way more interesting than Marcus Aurelius' Meditations

62145579
>>62145009
Correct
>t. full trust that I'm where I need to be when I need to be there

62145590
>>62145579
retardmaxxing with extra steps

62145626
>>62145590
I wish I could impart to you even 1% of the things I've seen, alas just know its sort of like a Chinese finger trap you know what I mean?
>retardmaxxing
Okay, okay! Based!

62145653
>>62143054
i went all in on long distance ejaculation training, sucks to suck, peace i gotta catch a flight, there are some ceilings that need coating

62145752
>>62145005
rich dad poor dad taught me to put all my money in pokemon cards

62147432
Izzat farming thread
Abandon ship

62147439
It costs zero dollars to not be so darned rude

62147451
>>62143054

fuck i forgot about pomodoro. imagine having a faggy little tomato timer on your desk as an adult

62148909
>>62144289
this

62151619
>>62144395
... if you want to end up in hell

62151984
>>62144435
Marcus Aurelius didn't trust the slut, he knew she was bad when he married her. Thing was, she was the daughter of the previous emperor who was a cool guy and Aurelius married her out of loyalty to him (plus he got to be emperor himself out of it).

62152046
>>62143054
for me its several different stimulants and an ssri
amazingly im still a husk of a human, its honestly impressive financially speaking

62152662
Scam

62152741
>>62143054
Many of those things have improved my life a lot. Of course, I am not a billionaire but I like to enjoy the little things in life

62152905
>>62145005
You're supposed to read good fiction and let your mind wander, not that (((self help))) slop.

62155066
>>62143054
tbqh deepest post on this board

62155320
>>62147439
opportunity cost of missing a zinger tho

62156116
stupid demoralizing thread

62156188
>>62152741
>Many of those things have improved my life a lot
like what

62156224
>>62144272
>>62144395
>t.

62156315
>>62143054
My coming has gotten out of control that I don't even try to do nofap anymore. From 16-21 I was extremely passionate about but now at 27 I unironically have multiple screens of degeneracy on play and I even plan out my sessions.

62156324
>>62143054
Ask yourself this. Do any richfag elitists like Bezos do any of this?

62156689
>>62143054
This made me laugh so hard idk. I cant believe take things so seriously, it's sad

item.title: I'm rich and wealthy. AMA — 19 replies — 7.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
I'm rich and wealthy. AMA

Open thread • Replies: 19 • Updated: 2026-04-26 01:23 UTC

OP

I'm rich and wealthy. AMA

Early replies (first 3)

62156305
Are you white?

62156311
>>62156305
No, Hispanic.

62156347
>>62156311
Why aren't you white?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62156415
what is the difference between having money and having wealth? in your own words plz

62156428
I just sneezed. Are you talking about me?

62156436
I will suck your latina cock for 100k.

62156452
>>62156311
How do I avoid ending up hispanic?

62157010
>>62156299
what do i stack

62157018
>>62156311
El Mestizo. Matalo.

62157020
>>62156299
describe the story of your life in 3 sentences. First is your childhood, second is your transitory stage between childhood to adulthood, and third is your adulthood up to your current situation.

62157022
>>62156347
Based asker
Fuck brown people they only waste their wealth on nigger tier shit

62157024
>>62157022
>>>62157020

my impression of him will depend on how he answers my question

62157051
I'm OP's hermano and he's fucken dead. Turns out he stole 10 ks of coke from the sinaloans. They didnt like that.

62157105
>>62157020
My mom was a hoe and all the men she was fucking physically abused and belittled me constantly. She married one and it was the same story but I outgrew him around 14 and the ensuing fight had me kicked out. Neeted with grandma working a shitty job here and there and married and divorced somewhere along that decade.

62157138
>>62156299
Low 7 figure hell?
Mid 7 figure purgatory?
High 7 figure limbo?
8 figure salvation?
9 figure ascension?

62157142
>>62156311
So you're rich, but not wealthy

62157221
>>62157105
You're not OP, and if you somehow are OP on a different device nowhere in there do you mention how you because "wealthy" unless its implied that your mom took it in the divorce?

62157237
>>62157221
I’m not op or wealthy.

62157242
>>62157237
I was replying to OP you retard

item.title: Sigh. So McDonald’s did it. They released the McValue® menu where each item is under $3. This new gimmick replaced the p… — 46 replies — 7.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Sigh. So McDonald’s did it. They released the McValue® menu where each item is under $3. This new gimmick replaced the p…

Open thread • Replies: 46 • Updated: 2026-04-25 21:41 UTC

OP

Sigh. So McDonald’s did it. They released the McValue® menu where each item is under $3. This new gimmick replaced the previous “Buy One, Add One for $1” menu AKA the only reason to have the app anymore after the deals turned to shit. I’ve also noticed with this update the drink prices increased by $0.20, a $0.40 increase YoY in my area. Yesterday I was starving after work but drove straight passed McDonald’s out of pure anger. Went home and ate some eggs and toast.

Thoughts?

Early replies (first 3)

62155716
>>62155708
Give it up, brother. Your fat ass doesn't need to be eating McDick's anyway.

62155723
>>62155708
For me, it's Wendy's. They still have a decent meal deal although it has gone up $1 over the past couple years. Ironically they have been a better value than Micky d's for a little while now.
>>62155716
Sounds like projection tb.h

62155728
>>62155708
learn to cook

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62155841
>>62155819
>>62155831
>>62155832
Projection.

62155851
>>62155748
To be fair, that's canadian dollars which are worth much less than usd

62155867
>>62155851
Canada and Australia should adopt a different dollar sign. They casually post prices in their shitty cheap plastic "dollars" on the internet like it's USD, and that confuses everyone.

62155874
>>62155708
>Yesterday I was starving after work but drove straight passed McDonald’s out of pure anger.
the goyim have gone insane

62155922
Anyone still eating McDonald’s can go get McFucked. I remember eating there as a kid in the 80s and they used to have events where some pedo used to larp as Ronald McDonald. The playground and food was great too. It was wholesome. The jews ruined that, along with everything else. Pizza Hut was also top tier. Fast food doesn’t deserve our money anymore.

62155943
>>62155708
"Product"

62155947
>>62155922

62155976
>>62155922
>Fast food doesn’t deserve our money anymore.
while the quality of ingredients at least outside the US has improved massively over the years, the overall price-to-value ratio has reached a point where you can get proper food at a restaurant for sometimes less but more often than not the same amount of money.
it just doesn't make sense anymore and too much of it is still bad for you so I guess I agree with you there.

62156013
McD is like the only cheap fast food place at this point.

$7.59 for a large meal. I just checked Wendys for a similar meal and they charge $11.59!

62156034
Will ozempic taken by every fatass in the country make mcdonalds drop their prices or will the influx of thinner people that think they can suddenly eat more make them raise?

62156037
>>62155708
It was $2.12 using the apps 40% off deal to get my one double cheeseburger because I didn't want two for $4, now it's $1.70. This works out better for me as long as they don't remove the app discount.

62156039
>>62155708
ill have 2 large fries no salt
4 mcdoubles extra onion extra ketchup add mayo
4 hot and spicy mcchickens extda mayo extra lettuce
40 mcnuggets all habanero ranch
big mac
uhhhhhhhhhhhh

62156052
>>62155737
>Top post on the FIRE subreddit is complaining about how much the price of taco bell has increased
If Taco Bell impacts your finances that much then you don't have enough to retire

62156090
>he didn’t take profits on the $5 meal deal
ngmi

62156160
>>62155732
Ok but why visit a real restaurant or at least some small business?

62156273
>>62155708
That fucking mcchicken used to be $1. Why is mcniggers still trying to call this shit a “value” menu.

62156289
>>62156273
yeah in 1980 dude. holy fuck for a finance subreddit this place sure does suck sometimes.
picrel is the menu around 1975.

62156291
>>62156273
honestly it should still be a buck, no way that sandwich costs more than 50 cents to make

62156292
>>62156289
the mcchicken was a dollar in the 2010's

62156294
>>62156292
sure, and rent was $500 and employees only made $4 per hour and insurance rates were cheap and franchising costs were cheap.

62156301
>>62156294
Why are you mad and wrong?
>>62156292
This poster is factually accurate. McD is crazy expensive now.

62156370
>>62155708
>crying that his goysyrup went up 20¢

62156472
>>62156160
Because I cannot be in two places at the same time. At least not in my corporeal form.

62156693
>>62155708
What happened to the DOLLAR MENU

Not the buy one and get one for a dollar I mean

THE DOLLAR MENU

62156721
>>62155716
I mean really. If the price of McDonald's is so high it bothers you then you're eating too much McDonald's, no way around it.

62156722
>>62155737
There has been a resurgence in smug girl posts with quality rebuttals

62156723
>>62155976
At this point it's a mouse trap

62156726
>>62155708
Fast food doesn't understand people eat their slop solely because its cheap. Probably for the better they price themselves out since people don't need to be eating that garbage anyways

62156731
These $5 meals are pathetic because I would need at least 2 of them to feel satisfied.

62156748
>>62155708
nothing on that menu should be over 99¢, and half of it should be less than 75¢
